mnemonics: STAR: Stretches, Turns, Alters, Rotates - the key geometric transformations on vectors in R²
keyPoints: A linear transformation T: R² → R² can be represented by a 2x2 matrix [a b; c d]
The transformation maps the standard basis vectors i = [1,0] and j = [0,1] to new vectors [a,c] and [b,d] respectively
Common geometric transformations include: scaling (stretching/compressing), rotation, reflection, and shear
commonMistakes: Confusing the order of matrix multiplication when applying transformations (composition order matters)
Assuming all transformations preserve angles and lengths (only orthogonal transformations like rotations do)
Forgetting that the determinant of the transformation matrix gives the area scaling factor
